author: @martinesimmonds5590
text:
Hello Robert,
Have the majority of your Pre-Production inventions, followed all the Laws of the accepted mechanisms of Physics and Molecular Science. A Mr Disinti, has renewed my interest into your inventions. I'm sorry if you have any distain for the 'New Wave' of science. Fortunately, Mr Disinti's papers can be found within the science community, he cannot be dismissed. Many, accredited Scientists are uncomfortable with Mainstream Science and its 'Laws'. Should We be thinking outside of The Box?

Can you confirm that for me please?  Thanks for all you do!!

author: @davidrathbone6978
text: Hi Rob with your ink you could make a postion sensor. Your axis moves a metal roller on the ink like a verable resistor and you read the position on your AtoD on your Arduino. This gives you absolute postion sensing rather than relative. Love your dip system. Look forward as always to you take on Chemistry.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I was thinking about this - it is a linear encoder you are talking about I think and I was thinking you would want to use the capacity qualities of the ink and use variable capacitance as the sensor value

replies.author: @davidrathbone6978
replies.text: I have used capacity thin film in the past for "smart" store shelves in the  USA the weight of the items simply change the capacity. The shelf was just paper with pictures of the product with just thin conductive film and a foam gap. The ink sensor I described before is just a strip of ink the length of the linear moment that simply changes its resistance by the moving arm. This is made today but is expensive. Your ink would make this work simply via reading the A to D value as the axis moves. The value will reflect the axis position.

author: @gr1f1th
text: tape a stick on the z axis and you could put three beakers per hotplate if you wanted to make more than one at a time. Looks like you could go up to six if you got crazy. :P

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @PressToDigitate
text:
I don't suppose you could use that to impregnate some conductive Goretex fabric (Nafion's cheaper cousin) with a layer of Iodized graphitic Carbon Nitride nanoplatelets, to test a "Poor Man's Fuel Cell" approach (that should work but no one has attempted properly)?


Or, in the layering of GaAs/GaSb/Bi2Te3 Photovoltaic -ThermoPhotoVoltaic -ThermoElectric laminates for >50% efficient total Heat-to-Power Conversion at high temperatures?  (Ref. JXCrystals, Dr. Louis Fraas, Seattle).  Hint: Mating his PV/TPV GaSb deep IR conversion with TE is the key to making Rossi/Parkhomov LENR practical for many near-term applications, including space power.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes I think you could mate

replies.author: @PressToDigitate
replies.text: Desmond Bagley Didn't happen.  "Skeptics" make up all kinds of fake debunkery; it seldom holds up to scrutiny.  Look up the time Richard Feynman killed somebody by disrupting a demo of the Papp Engine.

author: @craigglewis
text: Hi Rob, Tks for the update. I read that there needs to be 5 to 10 sec drying time before each dip? If you have to dip 500 times,  with a 20-40 sec cycle you are looking at 5 to 6 hours processing time?   Still waiting to get supplies. Canada Railway system has been shutdown with protests. So it looks like a bit of a delay. Yet looking forward to R&D of Graphene :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: just time the bar move to get the dry time mate and yes it would be 5-6 hours - but the contact time is low and this is lab kit really - we sent them mate so sorry to hear about the delay

replies.author: @tobinharris1973
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I'm guessing the reaction in each beaker is self-limiting because the insertion/extraction time is a substantial portion of the dwell time in each beaker. I this was a mere deposition, the bottom of the sheet would have more material then the top of the sheet.

replies.author: @yulin1037
replies.text: hello, could you still find the reference about 5 to 10 sec drying time before each dip?

author: @DanA-nl5uo
text:
Nice little walking beam setup.  You might want to consider a short vertical shake move at the top of stroke if you have a dripping problem.  
Are you just moving to limit switches?  Just wondering how you handled cumulative error over multiple passes in a setup like that limit switches seems like the easy solution.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes mate - limit switches - I talked about that in the first video on this I think
